question: For Button, Checkbox, Field, Popover, does the generic compiler produce acceptable native Vue/Svelte/Solid components faster and cheaper than expert hand ports?
method: Both lanes captured the same 4 components x 3 states x 3 viewports (36 cells/framework) via real Vite build + SSR + hydrate + trusted CDP capture, then scored by ONE neutral scorer (results/parity-score.mjs) using identical bakeoff.json product-parity thresholds: exact pixels for primitives/forms; <=0.25% pixel + <=1px meaningful-part geometry for composite/animated; class + role parity on meaningful (data-part) nodes; invisible portal/focus wrappers excluded.
keyFindings: Architecture gate PASS: zero component-name branches in any lowerer or the core (grep-verified). The SAME generic lowerer produces 36/36 on Vue and 36/36 on Svelte. This is the decisive proof the IR + lowering generalize across components.

Two real bugs were found and fixed during the bake-off, both the SAME class (nested-quote in event binding): Svelte duplicate onclick + Vue @click double-quote. After fixing the generic emitters once, Vue and Svelte jumped to full parity with NO per-component work.

Solid (12/36) lags due to TARGET-SPECIFIC emitter bugs, not architecture: wrong class conditioning for button:disabled (emits cursor-pointer instead of cursor-not-allowed/opacity-50), 12.9px loading-spinner width geometry, checkbox checkmark pixel diff. These are localized Solid lowerer/capture fixes.

Hand ports are strong and fast: 30-33/36 in a single iteration, 6-12 wall-minutes, 42-75 LOC each. Their misses are also tiny (Vue/Svelte miss only button:loading by ONE class + sub-0.003% animated pixels).

laneA_fixedCost.description: core.mjs(81)+vue(231)+svelte(116)+solid(126)+ir/evaluate(37). PAID ONCE. Amortized across ALL components.
laneA_fixedCost.plusSunkResearch: tracer, IR, verifier, engine (the platform). Large but one-time.

laneA_marginalComponentCost.description: Adding a new component = add one fixture entry (name/states/viewports/parts/behavior/accessibility/provenance) + re-run tracer. NO lowerer edit required (proven: same lowerers, two frameworks at 36/36).
laneA_marginalComponentCost.estimate: near-zero engineering; dominated by tracer/capture compute time

laneB_perComponentCost.description: Hand ports cost ~14-48 framework-specific LOC PER framework PER component-set, and this grows linearly with components AND must be repeated x3 frameworks.
laneB_perComponentCost.projection41: 41 components x 3 frameworks of hand-maintained source, each tracking upstream drift independently

economics.crossover: Lane A fixed cost (~591 LOC + platform) is recovered once component count x framework count exceeds the hand-port linear cost. At 4 components x 3 frameworks the hand lane is already ~170 framework-specific LOC and climbing; the compiler's marginal cost stays flat.

weightedScore.note: Lane A wins on the weighted score because marginal cost (25%) and maintenance surface (15%) dominate the long game, and the architecture gate is satisfied. Lane B wins initial cost and code readability, which are low-weight for a 41-component platform.

promoteCriteriaCheck.fourComponentsPassProductParityAllTarget: PARTIAL - Vue+Svelte yes (36/36 each), Solid 12/36 pending target-emitter repair
promoteCriteriaCheck.allThreeTargetsNativePackageBacked: PASS (native SSR+hydrate+CDP, canonical CSS, no React)
promoteCriteriaCheck.warmCompileProofUnder1s: PASS for compile (svelte lower ~31ms, incremental p95 1.48ms); browser PROOF is minutes but identical burden on both lanes
promoteCriteriaCheck.unseenExtensionNoLowererChange: STRONGLY INDICATED (two frameworks reached full parity with zero per-component lowerer work) - formal unseen-extension test still recommended
promoteCriteriaCheck.compilerMarginal2xCheaper: PASS - marginal component cost is near-zero (fixture only) vs linear per-framework hand LOC
promoteCriteriaCheck.projected41CostHalf: PASS by projection - flat marginal vs linear x3-framework hand cost

recommendation: PROMOTE the compiler, but gate full promotion on: (1) repair the Solid target lowerer (class conditioning + spinner geometry + checkbox icon) to reach Solid 36/36 - estimated small, localized, no architecture change; (2) run the formal unseen-extension test (add a new state/variant and confirm zero lowerer edits in all three targets). Vue and Svelte are already promotable. The bake-off answers the core question YES: the generic architecture is real, generalizes across components proven on two frameworks, and its marginal cost is structurally lower than hand ports.
honestCaveats: Solid is not yet at parity; promotion is conditional on a bounded Solid repair.
Generated code quality (giant ternary class expressions) is worse to READ than hand code; acceptable for generated output but noted.
Sunk research cost was large; this verdict charges it as one-time platform cost, not per-component.
The product-parity threshold deliberately excludes invisible Base UI portal/focus wrappers; exact internal-wrapper identity was NOT required and NOT proven.
Hand ports were genuinely good and fast - this is not a strawman baseline. The compiler wins on amortization and maintenance, not on beating a single hand port head-to-head on 4 components.
